member.name: T:Skybound.VisualStyles.VisualStyleProvider
member.summary: Extends the standard Windows Forms controls to properly support visual styles.

remarks.para: The standard Windows Forms controls do not draw properly when visual styles are enabled. For example, the TabPage control draws a gray background instead of the properly themed gradient background. Transparent controls placed on tab pages also draw this gray background, even when the tab page itself is properly themed. Furthermore, the Image property is ignored and no image is drawn on Button, CheckBox, and RadioButton controls.

The VisualStyleProvider class fixes these problems. When you add a VisualStyleProvider to a form, it will use its internal drawing routines to draw the controls on the form. This means that transparent controls will draw properly on tab pages, and images will be displayed properly on Button, CheckBox, and RadioButton controls. Also, controls that should have transparent backgrounds (such as Label and TrackBar, and read-only TextBox controls) are drawn correctly.
Here is a list of the control types that are improved by VisualStyleProvider:

item.description: Draws images when themed. Without VisualStyles, images are only drawn when the control is unthemed.*
Draws checkboxes in the list using a themed appearance. Without VisualStyles, checkboxes are drawn using a standard 3D appearance.
GroupBox*, Label, Panel, PictureBox, TextBox, ToolBar, TrackBar and UserControl
Draws backgrounds transparently when the controls are placed on a tab page.
Draws the tab buttons at any of four alignment values: Top, Right, Bottom or Left. Without VisualStyles, only Top alignment is supported.

remarks.para: *Note about button controls: The Button, CheckBox, GroupBox and RadioButton controls have a FlatStyle property that *must* be set to FlatStyle.Standard in order to be drawn by VisualStyleProvider.

**Note about tab controls: The current implementation of Themes in Windows XP does not actually support tab controls when the tabs are aligned to the right, bottom, or left of the control. In order to simulate this effect, VisualStyles draws an upright, themed tab control and then rotates it 90, 180, or 170 degrees. Because of this, the shading is a little off (it looks as though the light source is coming from the wrong direction).

member.name: M:Skybound.VisualStyles.VisualStyleProvider.EnableVisualStyles
member.summary: Enables visual styles for the current application if the feature is supported by the OS and the caller has the necessary permissions.

remarks.para: This method will enable visual styles for the current application only if the current security context allows access to unmanaged code.
See the class for more information about using VisualStyles in a partial trust security context.

member.name: M:Skybound.VisualStyles.VisualStyleProvider.EnableVisualStylesInScope(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Enables visual styles until the returned object is disposed if the feature is supported by the OS and the caller has the necessary permissions.
member.param: True if visual styles are enabled; otherwise, false.

remarks.para: This method enables visual styles for the current application. Visual styles can be disabled later by disposing the object that is returned.

EnableVisualStylesInScope is particularly useful when writing an Add-in, and it is unknown whether the host application has visual styles enabled. It is important to enable theming only for your Add-in.

As with , visual styles are only enabled if the current security context allows access to unmanaged code. See the class for more information about using VisualStyles in a partial trust securitycontext.

example.para: This example shows a form called AddInForm using visual styles.
example.code: Dim disposableObj As IDisposable Try disposableObj = Skybound.VisualStyles.VisualStyleProvider.EnableVisualStylesInScope(True) Dim addInForm As AddInForm = New AddInForm addInForm.ShowDialog Finally If Not disposableObj Is Nothing Then disposableObj.Dispose() End If End Try

member.name: T:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemePaint
member.summary: Provides static methods that can draw controls using visual styles.

remarks.para: The methods contained in the ThemePaint class allow you to draw your own controls or elements of controls using the Theme API.
This class provides these methods:

item.description: will enable visual styles for the current application when the current security context allows access to unmanaged code. See the class for more information about using VisualStyles in a partial trust security context.
can draw a control or an element of a control onto a object.
can draw the background of a control onto a object. This is useful, for example, for drawning the background of a tab page onto the background of a transparent label control.
returns a color that should be used to draw text, lines and backgrounds of themed control.
, and return information about the size of the margins used when drawing parts.

member.name: M:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemePaint.Draw(System.Drawing.Graphics,System.Windows.Forms.Control,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Drawing.Rectangle,System.Drawing.Rectangle)
member.summary: Draws a control or an element of a control that supports visual styles.
member.param: The object to draw on.
The to draw.
The class name of the control to draw (see for a list of class names).
The ID of the part to draw (see for a list of parts).
The ID of the state to draw (see for a list of states).
The bounding rectangle of the control.
Specifies the clipping rectangle in which the control is drawn.
member.remarks: Calling this method when visual styles are not enabled or not supported by the operating system has no effect.

member.name: M:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemePaint.DrawBackground(System.Drawing.Graphics,System.Windows.Forms.Control,System.Drawing.Rectangle)
member.summary: Draws the background of the parent of a specified control that supports visual styles.
member.param: The object to draw.
The to draw.
Specifies the clipping rectangle in which the background is drawn.

remarks.para: Call this method first when drawing a control that has a transparent background. That way, if the control is placed on a tab page, rebar, or another control that has a special themed background, it will draw the background properly.
Calling this method when visual styles are not enabled or not supported by the operating system has no effect.

If the background color of the control is not SystemColors.Control or SystemColors.Transparent, the background is filled with the background color of the control instead. You can force the background to be drawn transparently by using the other overload of DrawBackground.

example.para: This example overrides the method of a that has a transparent background. When visual styles are enabled, the background is drawn using the Theme API. Otherwise, the background is cleared to the background color of the control. The transparent background will draw correctly on tab pages, rebars, or other controls that have specifed themed backgrounds.

member.name: M:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemePaint.DrawBackground(System.Drawing.Graphics,System.Windows.Forms.Control,System.Drawing.Rectangle,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Draws the background of the parent of a specified control that supports visual styles, and specifies whether to ignore the background color of the control.
member.param: The object to draw.
The to draw.
Specifies the clipping rectangle in which the background is drawn.

True if the background should be drawn regardless of the background color of the control; otherwise, false, and the background is only drawn if the background color of the control is SystemColors.Control.

remarks.para: Call this method first when drawing a control that has a transparent background. That way, if the control is placed on a tab page, rebar, or another control that has a special themed background, it will draw the background properly.
Calling this method when visual styles are not enabled or not supported by the operating system has no effect.

example.para: This example overrides the method of a that has a transparent background. When visual styles are enabled, the background is drawn using the Theme API. Otherwise, the background is cleared to the background color of the control. The transparent background will draw correctly on tab pages, rebars, or other controls that have specifed themed backgrounds.

member.name: M:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemePaint.GetForeColor(System.Windows.Forms.Control,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the foreground color of text for a specified control that supports visual styles.
member.param: The whose foreground color is returned.
Specifies the class of control.
The ID of the part for which the foreground color is returned.
The ID of the state for which the foreground color is returned.
member.returns: The text color, or if the color could not be obtained.

remarks.para: Each theme can specify a color used to draw text on each type of control, depending on the control's state. Use this method to get the color used to draw text on a specific control in a specific state.
Calling this method when visual styles are not enabled or not supported by the operating system returns Color.Empty.

member.param: The for which the color is returned.
Specifies the class of control.
The ID of the part for which the color is returned.
The ID of the state for which the color is returned.
The color to retrieve.
member.returns: The color, or if the color could not be obtained.

member.name: T:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemeInformation
member.summary: Provides information about whether visual styles are supported on the system and whether they are enabled in the current application.

remarks.para: In a custom drawing routine, use this class to determine whether visual styles are enabled in the current application before drawing. When they are not, you should use your own custom drawing code.

The VisualStyles assembly requires permission to call unmanaged code in order to use the Theme API. When loaded in a partial trust security context (for example, when the application was launched from Internet Exploerer or from a network share) access to unmanaged code is not allowed. Therefore, this class will report that visual styles are not enabled. However, there are by default no such restrictions for assemblies that are installed in the Global Assembly Cache. To use VisualStyles in a partial trust security context, install it into the Global Assembly Cache first.

example.para: This example draws the background of a button onto a Graphics surface. When visual styles are enabled, the button is drawn using the Theme API. Otherwise, it is drawn using the DrawButton method of the framework ControlPaint class.

member.name: P:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemeInformation.VisualStylesSupported
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the system supports visual styles.
member.value: if the system supports visual styles; otherwise, .

member.name: P:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemeInformation.VisualStylesEnabled
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether visual styles are enabled for the current application.
member.value: if visual styles are enabled for the current application; otherwise, .
member.remarks: If visual styles are enabled or disabled in Control Panel/Display Properties while your application is running, the value of this property will change.

member.name: P:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemeInformation.CommonControlVersion
member.summary: Gets the version of the Windows Common Controls DLL to which this application is linked.
member.value: A object representing the version of the Windows Common Controls DLL to which this application is linked.

member.name: T:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemeParts
member.summary: Provides a set of constants that define the parts of a theme that can be drawn.

remarks.para: Each theme class has many parts that can be drawn. For example, the StatusBar theme class has StatusPane, StatusGripperPane and StatusGripper parts that can be drawn seperately. You can also specify the state of each part using the fields of .
Use the fields in this class when calling the and methods of when drawing a control.

member.name: T:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemeClasses
member.summary: Each field of the ThemeClasses class is the name of a class that can be drawn by the Theme API.
member.remarks: Use the fields in this class when calling the method of to draw a control.

member.name: T:Skybound.VisualStyles.VisualStyleLinkLabel
member.summary: Represents a link label control that properly supports visual styles.

remarks.para: This control must be used instead of the standard control when used on a control with a themed background, such as a tab page. Unfortunately, due to the way the LinkLabel control is drawn, it is not possible to override the default background drawing behavior without createing a new control that derrives from LinkLabel.

member.name: T:Skybound.VisualStyles.ThemeStates
member.summary: Provides a set of constants that define the states of theme parts.

remarks.para: Each theme class has many parts that can be drawn. For example, the Button theme class has ButtonPushButton, ButtonCheckBox and other parts that can be drawn seperately. You specify the state of each part using the fields of ThemeStates. For example, the PushButtonHot states is used to draw a button in a "hot" state (the mouse is positioned over the control).
Use the fields in this class when calling the and methods of when drawing a control.
